_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of risk quantification frameworks, including Value at Risk, expected loss models, Monte Carlo simulation, and sensitivity analysis methods.
comments: DCWF ksa superset of WIDAI RM domain. RM-K-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: SP-K-009 (SP, cosine: 0.644), AG-K-010 (AG, cosine: 0.615).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.4/10 (raw 0.640). Shared vocabulary: [knowledge, risk]. DCWF-only terms [assessment, threat] vs WIDAI-only terms [analysis, carlo, expected, frameworks, loss, methods] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.913 (P=0.951, R=0.877). P exceeds R by 0.074 — the DCWF element's 5 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 19 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "Knowledge of risk threat assessment." and "Knowledge of risk quantification frameworks, including Value at Risk, expected l" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.659 ≈ STS 0.640 (gap 0.019). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [knowledge, risk] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 15.0%. Shared tokens: [knowledge, risk]. DCWF-only: [assessment, threat]. WIDAI-only: [analysis, carlo, expected, frameworks, loss, methods].
